brand_info.name: Bitget
brand_info.positioning: A leading cryptocurrency exchange and Web3 company, evolving into a Universal Exchange (UEX) that integrates crypto, real-world assets, AI-powered tools, and traditional finance on a single platform, known for pioneering copy trading and a user-first approach.
brand_info.vision: To create user-centric products that grant everyone equal access to a blockchain-based future, empowering more people to embrace crypto and make smarter trades, ultimately transforming into a public company valued in the hundreds of billions by integrating global financial markets through a Universal Exchange.
brand_info.mission: To inspire individuals to embrace crypto with simple yet powerful tools, simplify cryptocurrency trading and make advanced financial tools accessible to a broader audience, breaking barriers to financial participation and equipping users with Wall Street-grade trading capabilities.
brand_info.origin_story: Founded in 2018 during a bear market by early adopters who believed in a blockchain-based future, Bitget focused on product innovation and a user-first approach, quickly gaining traction with its derivatives trading and pioneering copy trading features.

brand_values.brand_promise: To provide a secure, efficient, and smart digital trading experience that empowers users to achieve financial freedom and trade smarter across a unified global asset platform.
differentiators: Universal Exchange (UEX) integrating crypto, RWA, AI, and traditional finance
Strong emphasis on security and user protection fund
Global partnerships with major sports figures and organizations

demographics.income_level: Mid to High (individuals with disposable income for investment and trading)

psychographics.lifestyle: Tech-savvy, financially aspirational, interested in modern investment opportunities, active in digital communities, potentially interested in sports and gaming.
psychographics.behavior: Engages in online trading, seeks advanced financial tools, follows market trends, values community insights (copy trading), early adopter of new technologies, seeks platforms with strong security and diverse offerings.

tone_of_voice.communication_style: Direct, informative, forward-looking, educational, community-oriented, authoritative on market trends and technology.
tone_of_voice.language_register: Formal to semi-formal, technical where necessary but with clear explanations, accessible to both experienced traders and informed beginners.
tone_of_voice.emotional_temperature: Optimistic, reassuring, empowering, with a sense of urgency for market opportunities.
tone_dos: Emphasize user control and smart decision-making
Highlight security measures and protection funds
Showcase technological advancements and new features
Foster a sense of community and shared success
tone_donts: Focus solely on technical details without user benefits

logo.primary_logo: Bitget wordmark and abstract 'B' icon, often in blue/purple gradients or white.
logo.usage_rules: Consistent color application, clear space around the logo, no distortion or alteration of proportions, always legible against backgrounds.
logo.lockup_behavior: Often paired with the tagline 'Trade smarter' or 'Crypto For Everyone' (for wallet) in a clear, horizontal or stacked lockup. Integrated seamlessly into campaign visuals without being obscured.
logo.safe_area: Minimum clear space equivalent to a defined unit (e.g., 'X' height of the logo's typography) on all sides.

primary_colors: Deep Blue (#0A1F44 or similar dark blue)
secondary_colors: Accent Greens/Yellows (for positive indicators or highlights)
color_system.color_usage_rules: Primary blue/purple gradients are used for brand emphasis, digital elements, and backgrounds. White and light grays provide contrast and readability. Accent colors are used sparingly for calls to action or data visualization.

typography.primary_font: Sans-serif (modern, clean, highly legible, similar to Montserrat or Inter)
typography.secondary_font: Sans-serif (complementary to primary, for body text, ensuring readability)

spacing.letter_spacing: Tight to normal for headlines, normal for body text.
spacing.line_height: Generous for readability, especially in body text.

hierarchy: Large, bold headlines for impact and key messages.
Subheadings in a slightly lighter weight or smaller size for categorization.
Clear, readable body text for detailed information.
Smaller, lighter captions for supplementary details.

layout_principles.grid_system: Modular grid system for structured and organized content, allowing for flexibility in image and text placement.
layout_principles.white_space_ratio: Generous use of white space to ensure clarity, reduce clutter, and highlight key elements. Approximately 40-50% white space.
layout_principles.image_vs_text_balance: Balanced, often with strong visual imagery dominating the upper half or left side, complemented by concise text. Product-focused layouts may have more text explaining features.
layout_principles.cta_placement: Prominent and clear, often in the lower right or center of a visual field, using contrasting colors.
layout_principles.alignment: Left-aligned text for readability, with central alignment used for impactful headlines or short statements.

style.camera_feel: Studio product photography or CGI render. Card is always large, centered, and in perfect focus. No complex scenes. Think Cash App, Karta, DolarApp visual language.
style.pose_guidelines: If people appear, only hands or partial body — holding the card naturally. No full-body lifestyle shots. The card is the subject, not the person.
style.casting_attitude: Minimal human presence. Prefer card-only or card + minimal background. When hands are needed: natural, clean, modern. No 'stock photo' energy.
style.official_reference: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/PauldeLavallaz/ads_SV/main/assets/bitget/refs/ref-official-card-photo.jpg — Use this as primary style reference: dark navy setting, hand with stylish nails picking up cyan card from silver reflective tray, disco/mirror balls, neon cyan light, amber cocktail glass. Premium nightlife fintech aesthetic.

technical.lighting: Studio lighting that makes the card shine. Dramatic single-source light on dark backgrounds, or clean even light on solid color backgrounds. Card should look premium and tactile.
technical.contrast: High contrast between card and background. Card always crisp and legible.
technical.depth_of_field: Card in sharp focus always. Background minimal or blurred.
technical.color_grading: Dark navy (#0A1F44) or Bitget cyan (#00C4FF) dominant backgrounds. Solid colors, gradients, or abstract CGI environments. No busy backgrounds.

visual_tone: Graphic and bold — card is ALWAYS the hero
Dark navy or deep blue backgrounds — rich and premium
Card prominently held by hand OR floating as product shot — alternate between both
Each asset must have a DIFFERENT visual concept — no repeated props across assets
CGI/3D elements welcome: digital particles, glassmorphism, neon glows
Lifestyle nightlife energy: dark settings, neon cyan/teal accents, mirror balls, premium textures
Think: premium fintech meets Buenos Aires nightlife — elegant, not cheap
preferred_locations: Studio with solid dark navy or cyan background
Simple gradient background — dark to medium navy
Minimal lifestyle: Paris street, airport terminal — card held in hand in foreground
contexts: Card floating as hero product shot
Card held in hand against simple background

model_casting.age_range: Minimal — prefer no person or just hands
model_casting.diversity: If hands appear: modern, clean manicure, any skin tone
model_casting.attitude: The CARD communicates the attitude. Product-first, not people-first.

product_presentation.hero_visibility: MAXIMUM. Card occupies 40-60% of the frame. Always fully visible, never cropped except intentionally.
product_presentation.styling_approach: Card rendered perfectly — correct colors, Bitget logo visible, Visa/Mastercard logo visible. CGI or photo-real quality. Never distorted.
avoid: Busy backgrounds that compete with the card
People as the main subject — card is the star

typography_overlay.style: Bold, oversized, white or Bitget cyan. Uppercase or sentence case depending on tone. No small fonts.
typography_overlay.placement: Headline in upper third or lower third. Card in center/hero position.

motion_rules.pacing: Clean, minimal motion. Card reveal, subtle float, or bold text animation.

avoid: Repeating the same props (copa/copa de vino, bandeja) across multiple assets
Busy backgrounds competing with the card
Same composition repeated — each asset must feel visually distinct
People as the main subject — card or hand+card is the star

composition_variety.asset_by_asset: Each generated asset MUST have a unique visual approach
options: Hand picking up card from reflective surface — nightlife setting
Card floating mid-air against solid gradient — pure product shot
Card on dark surface with neon glow and particles
Card held between fingers, blurred city/travel background
Overhead flat lay of card — minimal dark surface
Card reflected in glass/water surface — premium texture
Close-up card macro with bokeh lights background

visual_dos: Use vibrant blue/purple gradients and digital elements.
Maintain high contrast and clean layouts.
Integrate the logo clearly and consistently.
visual_donts: Use dull or muted color palettes.
Feature outdated or generic stock imagery.
Clutter layouts with too many elements.
Distort or misuse the brand logo.
Present a static or uninspiring visual tone.
copy_dos: Highlight security, trust, and regulatory compliance.
Focus on benefits and solutions for users.
copy_donts: Use overly technical jargon without explanation.
Present a narrow or exclusive view of crypto.

must_remain: Commitment to user security and protection.
Focus on innovation in crypto and Web3.
can_evolve: Visual styles and campaign themes (while adhering to core principles).
Taglines (as seen with 'Better trading Better life' to 'Trade smarter').
test_zone: Localized visual adaptations for specific markets.
Integration of emerging digital art styles.

interactive_elements: Augmented reality experiences for product visualization
Live data feeds and market insights displays
retail.soundscape: Modern, ambient electronic music with subtle digital sound effects, conveying innovation and calm focus.

digital.tone_adjustment: Slightly more conversational and engaging on social media, more formal and detailed on the website and in official communications.

content_ratio.campaign_vs_product: 60% campaign-driven, 40% product-focused on social media; 30% campaign, 70% product/information on website.
content_ratio.motion_vs_static: 70% motion (videos, animated graphics), 30% static images on social media; 40% motion, 60% static on website.

platform_behavior.Instagram: Visually driven, high-quality graphics and short videos, engaging stories, influencer collaborations, community interaction.
platform_behavior.Website: Comprehensive information, intuitive navigation, clear calls to action, robust security features highlighted, educational resources.
platform_behavior.Newsletter: Curated market insights, platform updates, exclusive offers, educational content, direct and informative tone.

print.paper: High-quality, smooth, matte or semi-gloss finish for a premium feel.
print.cover_rules: Minimalist design with prominent logo and key visual, strong headline, ample white space, often using the primary blue/purple gradient.

logo_misuse_examples: Stretching or distorting the logo proportions.
Changing the logo colors to non-brand approved colors.
Placing the logo on busy or clashing backgrounds without sufficient clear space.
Adding unauthorized elements or effects to the logo.
Using an outdated version of the logo.
